governing_requirement: Freeze executable full-frame/crop/resize/pad/flip coordinate transforms with typed input/output spaces and dimensions, interpolation, rounding, orientation, side swap, parameters, canonical chain hash, inverse, and round-trip evidence; prove bounds, subpixel/tolerance, order, inverse, and protected-region failures close deterministically.

prerequisite.disposition: Do not implement or complete MF-P6-09.02 before the frozen identity prerequisite is adopted.

existing_enforcement.evidence: mask_acquisition_request.schema.json defines source/crop/output coordinate states, typed crop/resize/pad/horizontal-flip/project parameters, ordered steps, per-step and chain hashes, and a required round-trip policy.

maskfactory.validation:_transform_chain_issues checks chain/step hashes, zero-based sequence, unique step identities, exact contiguity, operation/parameter agreement, crop bounds, resize/pad/flip dimensions, finite invertible projections, source/output binding, and mandatory round-trip/noninvertible policy.

Current semantic validation binds executed-step hashes, coordinate spaces/dimensions, round-trip status/tolerance, artifacts, protected regions, receipts, and operational certificate coordinate evidence.
mask_bridge_semantic_invariants_v1.json names executable_transform_replay and requires typed contiguous dimensionally executable ordered round-trip transforms.

missing_proof: No standalone producer-owned executor applies the closed step sequence to points, boxes, binary masks, or protected regions and returns exact replay evidence.
No executable inverse builder proves crop/resize/pad/horizontal-flip/project reversal under one pinned rounding and subpixel-tolerance policy.
No implementation binds the actual replayed input/output raster hashes, executed step hashes, inverse chain, measured maximum error, and side-swap result into one deterministic evidence record.
Horizontal flip structure requires character_side_swap=true, but no bridge executor proves ontology-aware left/right label exchange together with geometry inversion.

Existing focused bridge coverage proves a sequence failure and schema validity, not exhaustive bounds, dimension, interpolation, padding, rounding, order, inverse, protected-region, and side-swap behavior across all operations.
Structural declarations alone cannot prove that the original target/protected pixels survive forward and inverse execution within the declared tolerance.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement deterministic executable and invertible bridge geometry over the frozen v1 request contract after MF-P6-09.01 is adopted, without mutating the v1 wire schema or the held identity worker paths.
implementation_packet.path_relationship_to_held_09_01: Exact allowed files are disjoint. The held 09.01 worker owns src/maskfactory/bridge/__init__.py and identity.py plus frozen v1 schema/fixture paths; those files remain excluded from 09.02 writes.
acceptance_criteria: Full-frame identity plus crop, resize, pad, horizontal flip, project, and inverse-project steps execute in exact declared order.
Every step verifies typed input/output spaces and dimensions before execution and binds canonical step and chain hashes.
Interpolation, padding, rounding, orientation, subpixel tolerance, and invertibility are closed policy values; unknown values fail closed.
Crop bounds, resize/pad dimensions, step order, contiguity, duplicate identities, and non-finite/singular projections fail deterministically.
Horizontal flips exchange ontology-defined left/right labels and reverse both geometry and side semantics exactly once.
Forward and inverse point/box/mask execution measures maximum round-trip error and rejects values above the pinned tolerance.
Target and protected masks bind exact source pixels, owner, transform, and authority and reject drift or protected-region mismatch.
Binary mask execution preserves strict 0/255 semantics and uses the pinned categorical interpolation mode.
Replay evidence binds input/output encoded and decoded hashes, executed step hashes, inverse identity, tolerance, result, reasons, policy hash, and canonical record hash.
Frozen v1 schemas, fixtures, semantic profile, catalog, and held MF-P6-09.01 paths remain byte-unchanged.
